visits.note: Patient P055 is a 65-year-old male with a history of Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. He presents with symptoms of fatigue and edema. Physical examination reveals 1+ pitting edema bilaterally. Labs show HbA1c 7.5, BP 150/95, eGFR 75, and LDL 140. Medications include Metformin 500mg BID, Lisinopril 10mg QD, and Atorvastatin 20mg QD. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. Symptoms include fatigue and edema.

Patient P055 returns with worsening symptoms of fatigue and shortness of breath. Physical examination reveals 2+ pitting edema bilaterally and a new murmur. Labs show HbA1c 8.2, BP 160/100, eGFR 60, and LDL 180. Medications are adjusted to Metformin 1000mg BID, Lisinopril 20mg QD, and Atorvastatin 40mg QD. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, and new diagnosis of Coronary Artery Disease. Symptoms include fatigue, edema, and shortness of breath.

Patient P055 returns with worsening symptoms of shortness of breath and increasing fatigue. Physical examination reveals 3+ pitting edema bilaterally and a new orthopnea. Labs show HbA1c 9.0, BP 170/110, eGFR 45, and LDL 200. Medications are adjusted to Metformin 2000mg BID, Lisinopril 40mg QD, and Atorvastatin 80mg QD. Diagnoses include Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, Coronary Artery Disease, and new diagnosis of Chronic Kidney Disease. Symptoms include fatigue, edema, shortness of breath, and orthopnea.
